risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "patients who are found to be eligible will be randomly allocated to the intervention or comparator arm of the trial by the automated randomization service operated by Nottingham Clinical Trials Unit. Equal numbers of participants will be randomly assigned to each arm of the trial. Stratification will occur by study centre, severity of personality disorder ... and extent of bipolarity ..." Comment: An automated randomization service indicates a proper random allocation process, and the use of stratification is consistent with a systematic random sequence rather than a predictable rule. This is sufficient for Low risk.

Quote: "patients who are found to be eligible will be randomly allocated to the intervention or comparator arm of the trial by the automated randomization service operated by Nottingham Clinical Trials Unit." Comment: This explicitly states allocation was done through an automated randomization service operated by a trials unit, which is a central allocation mechanism that prevents the recruiter from foreseeing the next assignment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The lamotrigine and placebo capsules will be kept in a suitable temperature-controlled environment at each site." and "Blinding of investigators, researchers, clinicians, and patients will be maintained until all data entry and processing are complete and the database has been locked. All patients, carers, and study personnel will be blinded to treatment assignment." Comment: The study explicitly states that patients and study personnel were blinded, with placebo capsules identical in appearance to active medication. This makes knowledge of allocation unlikely to have influenced delivery of care or participant behavior, so the risk of performance bias is low overall.

Quote: "Blinding of investigators, researchers, clinicians, and patients will be maintained until all data entry and processing are complete and the database has been locked. All patients, carers, and study personnel will be blinded to treatment assignment." Comment: Outcome assessment was performed by researchers, and the trial explicitly states that researchers were blinded. The main outcome (ZAN-BPD) and most secondary outcomes were assessed by trained researchers using structured interviews/scales, so knowledge of allocation was unlikely to influence measurement while blinding was maintained.

Quote: "The primary approach to between-group comparisons will be to analyse participants according to the group to which they were allocated, regardless of treatment received, and without imputation of missing outcome data." Comment: Although the protocol does not give the final per-arm dropout numbers here, it shows an intention for ITT analysis, active follow-up of all randomized participants, and prespecification of mixed models/multiple imputation for missing data. The study also planned only 15% loss to follow-up at 52 weeks, which is within the range usually considered reasonable. Overall, the report suggests attrition was anticipated and handled in a way that makes serious attrition bias unlikely.
